_comment: Governed binding map for the job-costing ledger: ties the three ontology
layers to the physical SQL ledger. EXPLICIT ONLY — same fail-visible rule
as binding_bridge.json: a concept or event class absent from this file is
simply unbound; the loader (hf-space-inventory-sqlgen/ledger_bindings.py)
additionally FAILS CLOSED if any entry here does not exist in its layer.
URIs use the compact 'ledger:' form exactly as they appear in
ontology/ledger_skos.jsonld and ontology/ledger_events.ttl
(ledger: = http://example.org/manufacturing/ledger#).
concept_table_bindings: SKOS concept URI -> physical ledger table.
Only the five concepts with a physical twin are bound; the raw-material
subtype concepts (Standards, Detail Parts, Components, Sheet Metal) are
vocabulary-only and deliberately unbound.
event_class_bindings: RDF (OWL) posting-event class -> gl_events.event_type.
One-to-one with the four posting event types; the loader cross-checks each
class's skos:closeMatch concept notation against the bound event_type.
entity_table_bindings: OWL entity class -> physical (non-gl_*) table + key
column. ledger:WorkOrder grounds 1:1 on work_order keyed by wo_id — this is
what lets the semantic layer resolve 'job 42' to a wo_id. The loader
fails closed if the class is not declared in ledger_events.ttl or the
table is absent from the governed graph.
